title: MigraLog CloudKit Schema v1
description: Defines the CKRecord types promoted to the CloudKit container. IMMUTABLE once in production: record types and fields cannot be renamed or deleted. Design principle: keep this minimal — CloudKit is a dumb sync pipe, not a queryable backend.

SyncRecord.description: Single CKRecord type for all synced data. Each local SQLite row becomes one SyncRecord in CloudKit.

tableName.description: CKRecord field (String). The local SQLite table this record belongs to (e.g. 'episodes', 'medications').

recordId.description: CKRecord field (String). The local UUID primary key. Also used as the CKRecord.recordName for deduplication.

payload.description: CKRecord field (String). JSON-serialized row data. Schema interpretation is client-side only.

schemaVersion.description: CKRecord field (Int64). Local SQLite schema version that produced this payload. Allows the client to handle migrations on read.

updatedAt.description: CKRecord field (Int64). Unix epoch milliseconds from the local record's updated_at. Used for last-write-wins conflict resolution.

deleted.description: CKRecord field (Int64). Soft-delete flag. 1 = tombstone. Allows delete propagation without losing the CKRecord.

_design_notes.why_opaque: CloudKit schema is immutable once promoted to production. By storing row data as a JSON payload, the local SQLite schema can evolve freely (add tables, rename columns, change types) without ever needing to touch the CloudKit schema.
_design_notes.record_name_strategy: CKRecord.recordName = '{tableName}:{recordId}' — guarantees uniqueness across tables and allows the client to parse the source table from the record name alone.
_design_notes.zone: All SyncRecords live in a single custom CKRecordZone ('MigraLogZone') in the user's private database. Zone-based sync gives us atomic commits and server change tokens.
_design_notes.conflict_resolution: Last-write-wins using the updatedAt field. CloudKit's built-in modificationDate is set by the server and reflects upload time, not edit time — so we carry our own.
_design_notes.soft_deletes: We use a 'deleted' flag instead of deleting CKRecords. This ensures the delete propagates to other devices via zone change tokens. Tombstones can be purged after a retention period.
_design_notes.data_safety: Data loss prevention is a core design tenet. Three layers: (1) Automatic local backup before first-ever sync on a device. (2) On conflict, the losing payload is preserved in the local sync_conflicts table for 90 days — restoring is just writing the payload back and queuing for sync. (3) Soft deletes in CloudKit mean no record is ever truly destroyed by sync. The existing backup/export system remains the nuclear recovery option.
_design_notes.future_expansion: New fields can be added to SyncRecord if needed (e.g. a 'priority' field for sync ordering). New record types can be added. Nothing existing can be changed.
